title: DevReview Ally
preview:
-- Company background:
- Seeded in 2020, builds a GitHub-style PR review assistant used by internal engineering and SRE teams.
- Focus: automated diff summarization, actionable comment generation, reranking candidate comments based on code context and style guides.

-- Primary ask for Redwood:
- Low-latency token-streaming inside IDE (VS Code extension) for near-real-time comment suggestion (target p95 < 100ms for token chunks).
- Deterministic PR summaries for CI checks (model/version pinning + reroll strategy).
- Reranking service for top-3 comment candidates, with adjustable scoring weights (security, style, performance).
- Support for multi-model routing: small fast model for short diffs, larger model for complex refactors.

-- Security & deployment constraints:
- Must run within VPC for customer repos that contain PII/credentials; enterprise customers require private deployment option.
- SSO via SAML, audit logging retained 1 year, KMS-managed keys for encryption, SOC2 compliance required.
- Data residency: EU customers require EU-only hosting.

-- Timeline / recent activities:
- 2026-02-11: Kickoff call (Fireflies ff_2026-02-11_DevReviewKickoff). AE Maya + SE Ethan joined. Demo of competitor Copilot integration.
- 2026-02-18: Sent initial security questionnaire (drive:/deals/devreview/pr-technical-reqs.md).
- 2026-03-02: POC checkpoint call (Fireflies ff_2026-03-02_POC-Checkpoint). Confirmed GitHub App webhook flow and sample repo.
- 2026-03-05: Engineering asked for sample latency traces and kernel selection notes (email thread_devreview_sales_20260210).
- POC milestones (customer-specified):
  * Week 1: GitHub App + VS Code extension integration, basic streaming enabled.
  * Week 2: Diff summarization accuracy run on 500 repos, measure F1 vs human summaries.
  * Week 3: Security review and penetration checklist (legal + infra).
  * Week 4: Scale test (200 concurrent reviewers, 300 req/s sustained) and cost estimate.

-- Technical preferences / details captured:
- Preferred model types: quantized LLaMA-family or Llama2-variants for balance of latency and quality. Customer asked about Redwood's verified model catalog and recommended quantization profiles.
- Model pinning required per-repo and per-branch (must support rollout/canary to 5% of traffic).
- Caching: aggressive prefix/KV cache for long-running threads; needs hinting APIs for repo-specific caches.
- Reranking: wants ability to plug in custom heuristics (security scanner scores) into rerank pipeline.

-- Quote from Eng Lead (paraphrased): "We need deterministic outputs for CI commentary — if the bot comments wrongly it blocks merges. Also want a fast fallback path so reviewers aren't blocked if model is busy."

-- Commercial notes:
- Interested in Dedicated for predictable latency with the option to move to Private for EU-only enterprise customers.
- Budget: internal estimate $50k-150k ARR depending on scale and retention.

-- Next steps / ask list:
- Provide Dedicated trial access and a sandboxed GitHub App for POC.
- Security deep-dive: SOC2 report + KMS integration docs.
- Latency benchmark runbook and recommended kernel/quantization settings for their workload.
- Legal to review data residency clause; request sample contract language.

-- Internal AE notes:
- AE Maya to coordinate legal doc handoff. SE Ethan to run two targeted latency tests week of 2026-03-15. Follow-up scheduled 2026-03-18.

title: BillMinder Systems
preview:
Top-line: mid-market billing SaaS (B2B) scaling support operations. Evaluating Redwood Hosted API as an initial POC to replace brittle webhook/rule stacks for subscription support agents. Plan: 2-week POC on Hosted API focusing on streaming chat latency and reliable function/tool-calling into Stripe sandbox. If POC proves latency and cost targets, move to Dedicated for predictable monthly throughput.

Key technical requirements (customer language / AE notes):
- Workload: customer support agents handling billing/subscription flows (refunds, invoice re-creation, plan changes). Heavy use of tool-calling to Stripe/Zuora sandbox; outputs must be strict JSON for automated downstream processing.
- Latency SLO: time-to-first-byte (streaming start) < 200ms for agent-facing flows; end-to-end JSON payload generation for tool call within 500ms median under 30 concurrent sessions.
- Throughput: expect bursts ~30-60 concurrent agent sessions during EMEA morning; monthly token estimate ~500k-1.2M tokens with heavy peaks during billing cycles.
- Cost sensitivity: wants unit economics scenarios for (a) Hosted with autoscaling vs (b) Dedicated reserved pools + burst pricing. Needs example invoice showing cost per 10k support interactions.
- Model preferences: prefer Redwood-curated open models with fallback to smaller quantized variants for low-risk flows (billing FAQs) and larger variant for complex disputes. Routing rules: latency-first for live agent flows, cost-first for async triage.
- Security/compliance: SSO/SAML required; audit logging and KMS for keys; data residency (EU for EU customers) important for regulated clients.

POC scope (agreed on 2026-03-11):
- Week 1: Setup Hosted API access, connect Stripe sandbox, implement simple function calling endpoint that accepts JSON schema: {action, invoice_id, amount_cents, reason, idempotency_key}. Validate idempotency and error modes. SE to provide sample code.
- Week 2: Simulated agent traffic (30 concurrent) with streaming responses; measure TTFB, p50/p95 token latencies, model cost per session. Collect transcripts and structured outputs.
- Success criteria: TTFB < 200ms p50; function call JSON schema matches spec 95% of the time; cost per 10k interactions within agreed threshold; no data leakage in logs; SSO demoable in staging.

Quotes / call notes: "If we can't get first-byte that fast we can't go live — agent UX will suffer" (Head of Support). "We need concrete cost numbers for peak month before procurement can sign" (VP Finance).

Next steps (short):
- Schedule deep-dive POC kickoff w/ infra and security (owner Jordan + SE Priya)
- Provide dedicated sizing worksheet and example Hosted vs Dedicated pricing scenario (AE to attach deck)

title: Auraborne VelocityWorks
preview:
Summary: Auraborne is an adtech SSP/RTB stack (header bidding + real-time personalization). They need sub-50ms p95 end-to-end inference for impression-time scoring and reranking, with peak bursts to ~6k rps and sustained 1.2k rps. Very cost-sensitive: want to evaluate batching, KV/prefix caching, and aggressive quantization to reduce GPU count and TCO.

Key requirements (from AE/SE calls):
- p95 <= 50ms for scoring path; target 30ms for experiments.
- Throughput: baseline 1.2k rps, burstable to 6k rps for 2-5s windows.
- Deterministic fallbacks: fallback routing to smaller quantized model when Dedicated pool saturated.
- Cold-start behavior: minimal latency for first request after idle (we discussed warm pools + micro-batching).
- Model preferences: want to test Llama2-13B-quant, Mistral-7B-8bit, and Redwood-verified 7B variants.
- Data constraints: ad payloads include user IDs (hashed). Must remain in customer VPC; logs retention 90d; require KMS integration + audit log export.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2026-01-20: Initial inbound demo request via website (AE: Maya assigned).
- 2026-02-03: Intro call (Maya/Ethan) — discovery; high-level perf targets captured. (ff_20260203_auraborne_intro)
- 2026-02-18: Security questionnaire submitted; SEC ticket opened (SEC-451).
- 2026-03-03: Detailed architecture review + perf expectations (SE deep-dive). Fireflies: ff_20260303_auraborne_1.
- 2026-03-08: Submitted POC scope doc + pricing (drive:/deals/auraborne/Redwood_POC_pricing_v2.pdf).
- 2026-03-11: Contract/legal flagged data residency clause; procurement loop started.

POC plan (agreed, 4 weeks):
- Week 0: Access & baseline: deploy Dedicated pool in us-east-1 VPC, ingest sample RTB logs (~24h). Run baseline Llama2-13B float model.
- Week 1: Batching + KV caching: test continuous batching and prefix caching for impression scoring. Measure p50/p95, tokens/sec, and cost/token.
- Week 2: Quantization sweep: run 8-bit, 4-bit and structured quant variants. Evaluate QoE (A/B on rerank metric) vs latency/cost.
- Week 3: Routing + fallbacks: configure smart routing (latency-first vs cost-first) and test failover to smaller models under load. Cutover dry-run with synthetic bursts.
- Deliverables: perf dashboard (per-route p50/p95, cost/token), recommended quant+batch profile, rollout playbook.

Metrics & success criteria:
- p95 inference <= 50ms (goal 30ms) for top-3 scoring requests.
- Cost per 1M impressions reduced by >= 30% vs current on-prem GPU baseline.
- No PII leakage; pass security checklist (SOC2 items + KMS + audit log export).

Notes from calls / quotes:
- \"If we can't get sub-50ms reliably at 6k bursts, we can't sell this product to our largest exchange partners.\" — Head of Platform (call 2026-03-03).
- SE feedback: strong candidate for batching + KV prefetching because many impressions share repeated prefix context.

Technical concerns raised by Auraborne:
- Cold-start behavior when Dedicated pool scales to zero during night; wants clear warm-pool strategy.
- Quantization risk: worried about rerank quality regressions — asks for eval harness with A/B scoring.
- Audit/tracing: needs per-request trace IDs to reconcile with bidding logs.

Action items / next steps (short):
- SE to schedule 30m perf workshop w/ infra + platform teams (Ethan) — week of 2026-03-15.
- Provide sample perf harness + synthetic burst generator (SE deliverable).
- Legal to propose data residency amendment for VPC-only logs export.
- Procurement: push term sheet; pending signature.

Attachments / refs:
- Drive: Redwood_POC_pricing_v2.pdf (pricing + capacity options).
- Security questionnaire XLSX (drive:/security/auraborne/Security_Questionnaire_auraborne.xlsx).
- Jira tickets: INF-1223 (initial infra ask), SEC-451 (security gaps).
- Gmail thread: thread:1758234b1d (pricing back-and-forth).

Short CRM-style items (quick view):
- Fit: strong (latency-critical, high volume, cost-sensitive).
- Likely product: Dedicated + Optimize recommendations; Private option for strict VPC/residency.
- Risk: legal/data-residency + quantization quality.
- Win-plan: fast perf POC, quantization A/B, show >=30% cost savings and p95 SLO.

Misc shorthand entries:
- AE notes: pref to bill quarterly; wants pilot discount.
- SE notes: perf harness expected by 2026-03-14.
- Procurement: SLA questions on scheduled maintenance windows.

title: AxiomFlux RAGServe
preview:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (customer support KB) evaluating Redwood hosted API for an embeddings + ANN frontend and cross-encoder reranker. Priority = high QPS + predictable cost.

Context and needs:
- Indexing scale: ~1.0-1.4M canonical docs (mix of articles, transcripts), ~20M embedding vectors expected after passage-splitting. Daily delta ~3-5k docs.
- Query profile: customer-facing help widget + internal agent assist. Peak QPS target 500-650 qps, baseline ~250-350 qps. 95th-tail SLA request: sub-150ms response for retrieval-only; end-to-end (retrieval + rerank) target <350ms.
- Cost sensitivity: want working cost model. Ballpark target <$0.001 per served user query (prefer ~ $0.0005 - $0.0008) including embedding retrieval + rerank.
- Quality: first-pass ANN recall top-100 then cross-encoder rerank for final top-5. Need support for dense retrieval + on-the-fly reranking.
- Feature asks: incremental index updates, TTL/freshness tuning, partial residency (EU subset), request-level audit logging, SSO (SAML) for admin console, KMS integration for keys.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (AE Ethan) — high-level product fit, asked for rough perf numbers and pricing.
- 2025-12-05: Architecture deep-dive with SE (Rina) — validated pipeline idea (ANN -> cross-encoder). Action: Redwood to propose POC config.
- 2026-01-18: Security screening kickoff — customer provided compliance checklist. Legal: wants SOC2 in writing + data residency options.
- 2026-02-04: Internal sizing workshop — AxiomFlux estimated 1.2M docs; Rina suggested batching + prefix caching.
- 2026-03-03: Demo of hosted API (small sample) — customer liked latency and SDKs but asked for scaled run. Fireflies: ff_2026_3249_axiomflux_meeting.
- 2026-03-10: Pricing scoping call — customer requested a cost model for 300 qps and for a 600 qps bursting scenario.

POC ask / success criteria:
- Redwood runs a perf POC on hosted API using a representative 1M-doc index (customer uploads sanitized sample).
- Measure: retrieval-only (ANN) 95th < 150ms at 500 qps; retrieval+rerank (top-100 -> cross-encoder) end-to-end <350ms at 300 qps.
- Cost calc delivered: $/query breakdown (embeddings + ANN + reranker) and optimization recommendations (quantization, batching, cache TTLs).

Customer quotes and sentiment:
- "We need the widget to feel instant for customers but can move heavy reranking to agent side if tails don't behave."
- Product lead: "If you can show <0.8ms/k token cost improvement w/ caching & batching we'll move forward." (paraphrase)

Technical notes / constraints:
- Preferred models: open-weights embedding model + a cross-encoder for rerank (customer open to Redwood model recommendations).
- They prefer to keep documents indexed in public cloud (AWS) but require EU residency for EU-customer content.
- PII scrub pipeline planned; legal will require proof of audit logging and retention controls.

Next steps (short):
1) Schedule hosted API perf POC run window (Rina + infra team) — target week of 2026-03-24.
2) Send tailored pricing deck + breakdown by component (embeddings, ANN, rerank). Link: /drive/pricing/axiomflux-pricing-deck-v2.pdf.
3) Respond to security checklist w/ SOC2 evidence and KMS integration notes — attach security FAQ (https://drive.redwood.com/security/axiomflux-security-faq.pdf).

Open blockers (detail):
- Legal: confirmation of SOC2 scope and timeline for attestation (customer legal requires before signing).
- Residency: need a plan for partial EU-only index + confirm data egress controls.
- Perf/cost proof: customer wants measured POC results at 1M docs at representative QPS before committing to dedicated capacity.

Notes on engagement tone: ongoing, pragmatic. Customer likes engineering-first conversations. Not price-led only; performance and controls are decision drivers.

Attachments & refs: pricing deck, security FAQ, Fireflies transcript id ff_2026_3249_axiomflux_meeting, Jira RWD-11234 (request to scale test infra).

title: Catalyst Haven Systems
preview:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics) — looking to replace slow in-house reranker and move support agent → semantic doc-search. Started on Redwood Hosted API; results promising for recall but unit cost and rate limits are a concern for scaled product usage. Team prefers to evaluate Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) to get predictable throughput and lower per-token costs.

Key POC asks / technical requirements:
- Workload: hybrid (real-time chat/support-agent latency sensitive ~ < 300ms p95; batch reranking for search results at 200 qps peak).
- Model preference: prefer curated open models; want a quantized 13B/7B variant option (Llama-like 13B-quant / 7B-quant) for cost-performance tradeoffs.
- Routing/fallback: POC must include canary rollout (5% -> 25% -> 100%), plus automatic fallback to Hosted API for overflow or regional outage. They want documented rollback triggers (latency > 350ms p95, error rate > 1% or quality regression on eval set).
- Regions: primary US-West, failover EU-West for users in EMEA, data residency for EU customers must stay in EU region.
- Security: SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration for keys, retention controls (30/90 day retention policies).
- Networking: VPC peering required for Private control plane; egress restrictions in some customer tenants.
- Observability: token-level cost breakdown, per-route latency, and sample outputs for QA.

POC plan (agreed):
1) Week 0: infra prep — provision Dedicated pool (2xA10 equivalent), VPC peering, service account and KMS test.
2) Week 1: Deploy model variants (13B-quant as primary, 7B-quant as cheap fallback), enable KV prefix cache for support flows.
3) Week 2: Canary traffic (5%) routed from production via feature flag; routing policy: route based on tenant and latency SLO preference. Collect eval metrics + human eval on sample prompts.
4) Week 3: Increase canary (25%), run cost/perf analysis and run rollback tests (simulated GPU outage) — verify region failover to EU and fallback to Hosted API.
5) Week 4: Finalize SLOs, document runbook for rollback and capacity alerts, pricing/commercial term negotiation.

Stated concerns from Eng/Product: "We need deterministic cost numbers before committing"; "If the Dedicated pool degrades, must not break customer-facing chat - fallback must be seamless".

Artifacts / references: Fireflies call FF-12903 (design session), pricing deck in Drive, security questionnaire (link below). SE notes: Lina to provide canary routing example policy and a JSON of rollback thresholds.

title: Copperlark Software Solutions
preview:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (product analytics) building a contextual support + doc search assistant. Narrowing path: start Hosted API to validate feature, then shift to Dedicated for predictable throughput and cost. Team is cost-sensitive and cares about p95 latency and unit economics.

Recent convo highlights:
- CTO (R. Meyer): "We need consistent 50ms p95 for UI snappiness; can tolerate quantization if quality stays within threshold."
- PM: concerned about monthly token bill spikes, asked for batching/caching levers and concrete cost model.
- SE asked: prefer models with KV cache friendly profiles; want recommendations for quantization profiles that preserve entity extraction accuracy.

POC details:
- Workload: user-facing chat + background reranker (embeddings) for doc search. Peak: 220 RPS during internal load tests; expected steady 40-70 RPS.
- Targets: p95 < 60ms (interactive), 95% of queries served under 80ms. Throughput budget: sustained 100k tokens/day.
- Initial model: Llama-2 style open model variant (customer-preferred), fallback to smaller quantized variant for low-priority queries.

Cost optimization workstream (what we ran / proposed):
- Batching: showed 2.1x token throughput improvement in hosted POC with small batching window (10-20ms). Tradeoff: adds 6-10ms median latency, reduces token cost ~28%.
- Caching: prefix + KV caching for FAQ-like queries cut repeated tokens by ~18% on their corpus; recommended TTL buckets by query type.

title: Amberfall Lattice
preview:
Account summary: Amberfall Lattice (mid‑market SaaS: product search for e‑commerce catalogs). Evaluating Redwood hosted API for quick embed generation + semantic search integration, then Dedicated for predictable throughput and VPC. Key ask: scale nightly batch embedding of ~150k docs and support 200 QPS vector lookups with <80ms median retrieval. Prefers open model families (Llama2/compatible) and quantized 8-bit variants; wants automatic fallback to smaller model during peak hours.

Sales/POC notes: demo 2026-03-03 showed Hosted API latencies acceptable for dev traffic; concerns around token costs for embeddings at scale. POC planned to run 2026-03-15 — 3 day Dedicated benchmark to validate: batch ingest speed, streaming latencies, GPU utilization, and connector idempotency. Quote requested: committed throughput pricing + burst rates.

Security: needs SOC2 attestation, SSO/SAML + SCIM, KMS integration for key control, audit logs retention 1 year, data residency in NA. "We need an audit trail for embeddings and must be able to rotate keys" — CTO Jordan Alvarez.

Integration: prototype uses Pinecone as vector store via Redwood embeddings; testing also with RedisVector. Wants a connector that can ingest in 10k doc chunks and support idempotent retries and resume on failures. Also needs sample autoscaling configs and cache TTL recommendations for prefix/KV caching.

Technical targets: nightly batch: 150k docs -> embeddings within a 6 hour window (~7k docs/min sustained); online: 200 QPS vector lookup with 95th pctile <120ms (end-to-end). Cost sensitivity: wants 20-30% unit cost improvement vs current managed embedding pipeline.

Blockers: legal needs dedicated pricing sheet; infra wants VPC peering diagram and network port list; SE asked for RedisVector throughput test harness and sample dataset.

Next steps: SE to deliver benchmark plan + infra network diagram by 2026-03-12; Sales to provide 12‑month reserved pricing options; schedule 2026-03-15 POC kickoff (Ethan + SE + Amberfall infra).

title: Archivio SearchWorks
preview:
- Vertical: enterprise SaaS KB for support teams. Moderate index size (1-1.5M docs ingest). High QPS expectation: target 300-600 qps steady-state for web-facing search.
- Primary ask: embeddings + reranking pipeline; low tail latency for top-K results (p95 < 200ms desired), keep cost-per-query < $0.005 if possible.
- Prefer hosted API (fast time-to-value) but want clear path to VPC/private later.
- Models: prefer small+fast embedding model (128-256 dim) + cross-encoder reranker for top-50 re-rank. Interested in Redwood "Optimize" suggestions (batching, cache, quantization).
- Security: SOC2 required, SSO/SAML mandatory, audit logs + KMS for encryption at rest. Data residency for EU accounts is a future requirement.
- Quote from CTO on call: "We need search that feels instant for customers — 150ms or bust for most queries. Cost needs to scale predictably."
- POC plan: index 1.2M docs (manual + HTML parsing pipeline), run simulated traffic 500 qps, compare vector DB pairing options (Pinecone vs. Milvus) and reranker compute costs.
- Internal notes: engineering liked Redwood runtime demo but asked for concrete kernel/quantization tradeoffs. SE to provide token-level cost breakdown and suggested batching thresholds.
- Risk: reranker compute dominates cost at high QPS; want fallback policy (approximate rerank or light reranker) to save $ during heartbeats.
- Next meeting: POC kickoff + security sync (2026-03-15).

title: BeaconFlow Labs
preview:
Account summary: BeaconFlow Labs is a mid-market B2B product company building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + micro-tips). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first to prove latency/cost, then committed interest in Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Key constraints / asks: 
- Latency SLOs: p50 target ~120-200ms, p95 target <500ms for interactive onboarding steps; streaming + function-calls must be consistent (users notice variance).
- Cost controls: token cost caps per onboarding session (target <= $0.15/session initially), need per-route token caps and throttles; interested in Optimize recommendations (batching/caching) to reduce unit cost.
- Tooling: must support function/tool calling to internal product metadata + CRM (tokenized access), streaming partial responses to UI while tool calls resolve.
- Security: SSO/SAML, SOC2 posture important, KMS for key management, audit logs retention policy. Data residency: US preferred for now.

POC plan: 2-week hosted run of 10k sessions simulated from production-like traffic, measure p50/p95 token usage, streaming latency, function-call end-to-end RTT. If pass, move to Dedicated quote and capacity sizing.

Call notes (shorthand):
- Elena: "Users drop off when assistant pauses > 500ms" -> strong need for consistent tail latency.
- SE (Ravi): suggested prefix/KV caching for repeated onboarding flows; showed batching configs.
- Procurement: price sensitivity on per-token for high-volume onboarding; wants Dedicated uplift only if unit economics improve or SLAs locked.

Linked artefacts: pricing deck, hosted-vs-dedicated TCO sheet, security FAQ, Fireflies transcripts (see linked_fireflies).

Next immediate actions: send hosted POC credentials, provide sample streaming config and recommended token caps per flow, schedule weekly POC syncs.

title: BlueHarbor Goodsight
preview:
Summary: BlueHarbor Goodsight runs a large e-comm catalog (approx 30M SKUs) and is evaluating a 2-stage semantic search + rerank pipeline to improve discoverability and conversion. Primary focus is embeddings for candidate retrieval (dense vector index) + a lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-10. Current infra is TF-IDF + heuristic rules; business wants better semantic recall without blowing up cost.

POC scope: 2-week pilot indexing 2M representative SKUs, simulate 1M q/day load, measure recall (NDCG), latency, and cost per query. They provided a product feed sample and session traces. Beta metrics target: candidate retrieval p95 < 120ms, rerank end-to-end (top-10) p95 < 200ms. Cost target: combined embedding+rerank budget $0.0006 - $0.001 per query at baseline; acceptable tradeoffs for short promo windows but need predictable ceilings for Black Friday (expected 6-8x baseline).

Security & ops: Require VPC deployment, SSO (SAML), KMS key management, audit logs and US data residency for payment/account data. Enterprise procurement will require an SOC2 report and retention controls.

Model preference & optimization asks: prefer CPU-friendly/quantized vector models for retrieval and a distilled cross-encoder for ranking; want Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching, quantization profiles, and KV caching to hit cost/SLO targets. Interested in automated fallback policies (cheaper model variant) during bursts.

Quotes / notes from last call: "If we can't get predictable cost during BF spikes we won't move off current infra." - Laura Morales (Head of Search). AE follow-up: send detailed cost model and example rollout for canary -> global.

title: CopperFalcon Insights
preview:
Customer: mid-market SaaS for customer support analytics. Current search stack: BM25 + embeddings (in-house). Pain: poor precision at top-k causing low deflection and higher agent workload. Evaluation path: hosted API first -> POC offline rerank on 50k queries -> small online A/B -> pricing discussion for dedicated if cost/throughput warrants.

POC details and asks:
- They want to use our rerank endpoint (offline scoring CSV) for head-to-head with their current ranker.
- Need clear instructions for eval set formatting (we provided template).
- Latency constraint: 95th pctl for single rerank RPC <200ms; acceptable to batch for bulk offline scoring.
- Cost sensitivity: targeting <$0.20 per user-active-month equivalent; looking to model unit economics for hosted vs dedicated.
- Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller model in case of capacity spike; also region-aware routing (US primary, EU data residency for some customers).

Security/compliance:
- VPC preferred, SOC2 required, SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS key management. Data residency: EU for certain enterprise customers.

Quotes / paraphrases:
- PM: 'We need a measurable bump in precision@5 without blowing up latency or cost.'
- Eng lead: 'If dedicated yields predictable throughput and cost parity, we'll seriously consider committed capacity.'

Next tactical items (short):
- SE to share dedicated pricing sheet + committed-capacity scenarios (by 2026-03-12).
- AE to coordinate legal for DPA and KMS questions.
- SE running extra offline runs to produce recall/precision curves per model variant (embedding + rerank model combos).

Notes: customer likes the offline scoring workflow; wants clearer documentation on canary A/B and rollback for Dedicated. Important to capture and surface token-level cost for rerank flows in Redwood Console for their finance team.

title: Cobalt Mesh Support
preview:
Account summary: Cobalt Mesh Support — mid-market SaaS (B2B support platform). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API as primary path with view to Dedicated in Q4 if volumes justify. Primary workload: chat-based support agents with external tool calls (search, order lookups, KB actions). Latency target: <=1.5s first-token on typical 64-token prompt; streaming required for live agent handoff. Concurrency expectation: peak ~200-350 concurrent chats, avg 80-120. Cost sensitivity moderate — wants predictable unit economics.
Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (AE: Ethan) — product overview, Redwood hosted features. Agreed to demo.
- 2026-02-02: Demo w/ SE (Maya) — streaming, function-calling walk-through. SLO discussion: "need sub-1.5s first-byte for US customers".

- 2026-02-25: POC scope agreed via Drive deck link; 48h perf window, synthetic + replay of 2k chat transcripts. Fireflies: ff_20260302_azq9.
- 2026-03-02: POC started (synthetic traffic). Noted spikes in first-token latency during bursts; SE tuning queued.
- 2026-03-09: POC mid-review — identified need for prefix cache and smaller model variant fallbacks; requested cost model for dedicated capacity.
Requirements summary:
- Streaming API + function/tool calls (required).
- First-token SLO: <=1.5s (US region) at 95th percentile.
- Auth: SSO/SAML for Console; API keys with KMS-backed rotation for Dedicated/VPC.
- Retention: 30-90 days configurable; audit logs needed for 12 months for admin actions.
- Residency: US West + East (priority), EU as future requirement.
Tech preferences/constraints:
- Prefer models with open weights or verified performance profile; quantization acceptable if quality preserved.
- Want automatic fallback flow: expensive model -> cost model -> smaller variant with degraded but safe responses.
- Will require per-route token/latency dashboards and tracing hooks for agent handoff.
Quotes/quotes:
- "If streaming isn't consistently sub-1.5s we can't go live." — Head of Product
- "We need concrete pricing scenarios: hosted at current volumes vs dedicated reserve." — Finance lead
Next steps:
- SE: run focused 24h perf run with adjusted batching and prefix cache; target replicates production mix (Maya).
- AE: schedule pricing review with finance for ARR scenarios (Ethan).
- Security: deliver completed security FAQ and KMS diagram (Rajat responsible).
Open items/blockers:
- Dedicated pricing proposal and burst policies.
- Security FAQ items about long term retention and EU residency.
- Validation of 95th-percentile first-token SLO under 350 concurrent chats.
Stubs/links: Fireflies ff_20260302_azq9; Gmail thread-GmCbl12345; Drive docs above; Jira INF-7892.
CRM notes: short, bulleted, some shorthand. POC treatment: evaluation -> procurement if POC meets SLOs and pricing aligns.

title: Aurora IDEWorks
preview:
Top-line: building a VS Code copilot extension (Electron + webview) targeted at SMB developer teams. Needs sub-50ms streaming token latency for inline suggestions; users expect immediate character-by-character streaming. Current prototype uses OpenAI stream but hitting rate limits and jitter. Want model pinning per-deployment (dev/demo/prod) and ability to hot-swap model versions without redeploying extension.

Key quotes: "We need streaming to feel native — no 300ms stalls when you type." — CTO Maya Chen.

Auth: plan to use OAuth + short-lived ephemeral keys issued by our backend. Concern: Redwood's rate-limiting semantics and per-user quotas; need guidance on token refresh and graceful fallback when hitting limits.

Data flow & compliance: extension sends small context window + workspace files hashed; embeddings for code search stored in our AWS account. Require SOC2, SSO (SAML), audit logs retention 365d, and optional EU residency for customers.

Perf POC requirements:
- sustained 200 concurrent users per instance
- 600 requests/min peak across team accounts
- median token latency < 60ms when using dedicated GPU pool
- burst capacity for live onboarding spikes

Integration notes:
- prefer websocket streaming with delta events; fallback to long-polling
- need SDK snippet for VS Code extension (TS) that handles reconnects, backpressure
- expected tokenization differences across models: need help mapping token counts to billing estimates

Commercial: prefer annual committed dedicated capacity for predictable latency. Target ARR in range above. Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching/caching to reduce unit cost.

Open items: confirm per-tenant rate-limit headers, recommended ephemeral key TTL, and example SDK code for streaming in VS Code webviews. Legal reviewing data residency clause (will share drive doc).

title: Cinderpath SaaSworks
preview:
Account background: Cinderpath is a mid-market B2B product analytics SaaS (user-facing product assistant + embedded code-assist feature). Currently using a major LLM API provider for chat/code assistant and embeddings. They have heavy token volume in user interactive chat (multi-turn) and periodic bulk reranking jobs for search.

Why Redwood: cost predictability at scale, better latency tail for their EU/NA customers, and desire for a single vendor that supports hosted then dedicated migration with minimal app changes. They asked specifically about Redwood's compat layer and prompt parity guarantees — worried about regressions when switching models/endpoints.

POC summary (Hosted API):
- Goals: 1) Verify prompt parity (no quality regressions), 2) Measure 95/99 latency and avg token cost, 3) Validate embedding similarity parity for product search.
- Setup: ported 3 canonical prompts + evaluation harness to Redwood Hosted endpoints; run A/B against existing provider over 48h window.
- Metrics being tracked: top1 ambiguity rate, hallucination incidents per 1k responses, avg tokens per conversation, 95th/99th latency, inference cost per 10k tokens.
- Early results (week 1): precision on rerank tests within 2% of baseline, embeddings cosine delta OK; avg latency down ~18% in NA, cost per 10k tokens ~12% lower on hosted config. 95/99 tails still under investigation for long-context API calls.

Technical asks from Cinderpath engineering:
- Redwood compat layer options (OpenAI-style endpoints) or light SDK shim to avoid rewriting client code.
- Controlled canary routing: start 5% traffic to Redwood Hosted during business hours only.
- Prompt parity tools: diff-run harness + automated regression alerts (they want fail-fast).
- KV cache persistence/behavior for chat state across retries and reconnects.
- Encryption/KMS and certs for Dedicated (timing for HSM integration important).

Commercial/Procurement notes:
- They prefer to start on Hosted with a short-term credit and then commit to Dedicated after a successful 3-month POC (expected Q2). Ask for a pricing schedule that maps to a 12-month reserved commit with burstable capacity.
- Procurement flagged need for a clear migration rollback plan and a definition of acceptable model divergence/regression SLA.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-20: Intro call - AE + SE - high level requirements captured (FF-20260225-7129).
- 2026-02-04: Demo of Hosted API + console; quick perf numbers shown.
- 2026-02-16: POC kickoff, shared evaluation harness and baseline dataset (Drive doc).
- 2026-02-18: Security questionnaire returned; asked for KMS and audit log samples (FF-20260218-6841).
- 2026-03-02: Mid-POC sync; embedding parity looks good, 95th latency variability noted in EU region.
- 2026-03-09: Pricing follow-up email from AE with 3 commit tiers; Cinderpath asked for a customized Dedicated estimate with specific GPU class and retention settings.

Quotes from stakeholders:
- "If prompt outputs diverge we lose product trust quickly — we need an automated regression gate before cutting traffic." — Head of Product.
- "Cost savings are meaningful, but uptime + predictable latency are why we'd move to Dedicated." — VP Eng.

Next steps (short):
- Deliver dedicated commitment pricing matrix by 2026-03-14.
- SE to run long-tail latency tests for long-context flows in EU region (end by 2026-03-20).
- Legal to provide redlined DPA + data residency clause; sync 2026-03-17.
- Schedule canary rollout plan review with CSM/Ops if POC OK.

Open blockers (notes):
- Need clear definition of "prompt parity" thresholds and acceptable delta for model outputs.
- Peering window: CIO requires VPC peering during maintenance window that currently conflicts with their nightly jobs.
- Legal: confirmation on subprocessors & EU data residency certification timeline.

Contact list: Ethan Park (AE), Maya Lopez (SE), Jordan Kim (CSM), CTO: Priya Raman (Cinderpath), Head of Product: Alex Mercer (Cinderpath).

title: DevStream.io
preview:
Summary: DevStream.io builds an IDE copilot product (VSCode/JetBrains) aimed at professional developer workflows. Main ask: enable ultra-low-latency streaming for inline completions and preserve smooth typing UX when network/compute contention occurs.

POC observations (shorthand):
- baseline streaming via hosted API: median TTFT ~120ms, visible jitter when multiple tabs/clients open.
- POC on small dedicated cluster: median TTFT ~45ms, but occasional spikes (100-200ms) when garbage collection/quantized model page-ins happen.
- Inline UX expectations: suggestions should begin streaming within keystroke latency budget (developer perceived TTFT ~ <50ms).
- Token budget tuning: want per-dev soft cap + backpressure semantics (degrade to smaller snippets, not hard failures). Example: 20-token quick completions inline; full-block completions use >100 token budget with explicit user gesture.

Technical asks / feature wishlist (from Priya + Marco):
- streaming chunk size control (stream every 4-8 tokens) and early token flushing to reduce TTFT.
- deterministic model pinning per customer + fast rollback to smaller quantized variant when load high.

title: Avenryx Platforms
preview:
Overview:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS (B2B), multi-tenant. Want to replace VendorX API for chat summarization + embeddings and move to Redwood Hosted first, then Dedicated once performance/cost targets validated.
- Primary ask: drop-in replacement for OpenAI-compatible flows, prompt parity, stable streaming, and identical behavior for stop sequences + function-calling.
Key non-functional requirements:
- Latency: 80th pctile < 350ms for 256-token chat requests in primary region.
- Throughput: burst to 70 rps for support-bot loads, steady 10–15 rps.
- Cost sensitivity: target <= 20% increase in monthly inference spend on Hosted during POC; Dedicated must show 30–50% unit cost reduction vs current committed pricing.
- Security: SSO (SAML), KMS integration, audit logs retained 1 year, EU data residency for customer EU tenants.
Migration concerns raised on calls:
- Tokenization differences: some prompts show longer token counts with Redwood model variants than VendorX; leads to marginal regressed outputs. Need tokenization parity check and recommended prompt rewrites.
- Function calling parity: VendorX handles nested JSON in tool responses differently; POC will include function-calling parity tests.
- Embeddings drift: small but measurable cosine distance shift on vendor switch for their fine-tuned embeddings. Want evaluation harness to compare retrieval hit-rate on canonical test set.
- Streaming behavior: partial delta ordering changes observed during demo; breaks client streaming parsers. SE to propose compatibility header or streaming mode toggle.
- Regeneration determinism: same prompt+seed returns slightly different summaries vs vendor. Investigate sampling/temperature mapping and top_p equivalences.
POC plan (agreed):
- Week 0: narrow scope, 10 prompts and 2 retrieval tasks chosen; share test harness (Drive link).
- Week 1: Redwood Hosted endpoints, run A/B vs VendorX on 50k tokens, collect metric set (latency, token usage, retrieval precision, user-acceptance).
- Week 2: Prompt parity fixes & rerun; escalate regressions to model/profile recommendations or warmed KV cache experiments.
- Post-POC: pricing conversation for Dedicated sizing (target: 4x T4 equivalent GPUs or Managed nv4 per capacity) and SLA.
Commercial notes:
- They are on a renewal with VendorX in Q3; looking to move before renewal if POC is clean.
- Procurement is sensitive to minimum commit for Dedicated; procurement wants a phased commitment with runway to expand.
Action items from last call (03/04):
- SE Ethan: provide tokenization parity checklist and lightweight shim examples for streaming compatibility (assigned, due 03/08).
- AE Maya: send initial Hosted pricing scenario + estimate for 2-week Hosted POC (sent 03/05).
- Security: Redwood security FAQ requested (Drive link).
- Avenryx to provide sample prompt set + reference retrieval corpus by 03/09.
Quotable lines from customer:
- \"We need a provider that won't surprise our product engineers — prompt changes can't silently change behavior in prod.\"
- \"If you can prove cost parity and fewer regressions, we'll consider Dedicated for next fiscal year.\"

title: Aurum Horizon Systems
preview:
Summary: Aurum Horizon operates route-optimization and real-time ETA services for large commercial fleets. They're evaluating Redwood Dedicated with multi-region reserved pools to guarantee throughput during peak routing windows and to meet contractual latency SLOs for enterprise customers.

Key customer quotes / snippets:
- 'We need predictable throughput for morning/evening load spikes — can't have degraded routing for 10k+ vessels.'
- 'Failover between us-east-1 and us-west-2 must be seamless; 15–20% headroom required.'

POC observations (short):
- Baseline (2-day): 120 reqs/s sustained on a mixed batch profile (avg payload 400 tokens); 95th pct latency for generation ~140ms using baseline A100 configs.
- Observed token distribution skewed: 60% short ( <128 ), 30% medium (128–512), 10% long (512–2048) — affects batching/windowing.

Commercial / procurement notes:
- Want reserved GPU pools across 2 regions (us-east, us-west) with Active-Passive failover; prefer mirrored capacity for EU pilot later.
- Target ARR band ~ $500k-$1M; 2-year commit preferred for pricing.

Internal ask from SE/AE:
- Need detailed per-AZ GPU counts and confirmation of headroom math for 99.95% availability target.
- Legal wants an explicit RTO for cross-AZ failover; procurement asked for credits table.

Stenography / misc:
- Drive links shared; awaiting answers to security matrix (drive:/security/aurum-security-questions.xlsx).
- Mentioned competitors evaluating: OpenAI enterprise endpoints and a self-hosted Kubernetes + Triton stack.

title: Arclytic Labs
preview:
POC goal: deterministic, reproducible code completions inside Arclytic IDE plugin with safe progressive rollouts.

- Background: Arclytic builds an in-IDE copiloting product used by internal dev teams + ~80 beta customers. Concern: unpredictable model updates changed completion rank/order and caused flaky build scripts. Wants model/version pinning + rollbacks.
- Key asks (from PM & platform lead): pin model version per release channel, reproducible sampling (seed), ability to run canaries split by user cohort, automated eval gates before each rollout.
- Acceptance metrics they care about:
  • compile-failure delta <= 0.2% vs baseline (reject if higher)
  • 95th pctl latency target < 120ms for inline completions (low-latency streaming is critical)
  • token-level quality regression threshold: no more than 3% BLEU/EDR drop on curated prompt set
  • rollback if user-reported error rate > 0.5% or automated guardrails tripped
- Desired rollout mechanics:
  • Canary at 5% of users (team-level flags), monitor metrics for 48h
  • Gate: automated evaluation harness runs on commit to model catalog; must pass quality + perf & return signed gate token
  • Gradual ramp: 5% -> 20% -> 60% -> 100% with automated rollback triggers
- Reproducibility requirements:
  • model_version stamped in completion metadata, session IDs persisted to logs and traceable to artifact store
  • deterministic sampling options for CI (temp=0, top_k/top_p pinned) and ability to replay sessions against older model binaries for bisecting
  • artifact retention: store candidate completions + inputs for 90 days (security needs opt-out)
- Integration points they want from Redwood:
  • SDK hook to pin model + sampling config at request time
  • per-deployment rollouts (dedicated pools) with canary routing and automatic fallback to pinned older model if latency spikes
  • evaluation-as-a-service: run their custom prompt set against model candidates and return pass/fail and diffs
- Timeline & recent activity:
  • 2026-02-18: initial intro call (Jordan) — high level feature interest, demo request
  • 2026-02-25: security questionnaire submitted (see Drive doc)
  • 2026-03-02: deep dive with infra + product (Fireflies FF-2026-03-02-8672) — confirmed need for VPC + KMS integration
  • 2026-03-05: demo of streaming and pin APIs (SE: Maya) — positive feedback; asked for canary flow example
  • 2026-03-08: legal sent initial data residency Qs; product requested POC acceptance criteria
  • 2026-03-14: infra workshop + POC kickoff (tentative)

- Quotes:
  • "We need deterministic completions for build scripts — model drift breaks CI runs." — Lead Platform Eng
  • "Canaries must be first-class, not a manual config we forget about." — Head of Product

- Risks: audit log retention limits may conflict with artifact retention; perf SLA on dedicated pools needs verification.

Short next steps (CRM): schedule infra workshop, deliver example canary rollout config + sample eval gate, confirm EU residency options for artifact storage.

title: Copper Falcon Dynamics
preview:
Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood. Initial onboarding via hosted API to validate latency/quality, scoped move to Dedicated (VPC) for production. Security review underway (SOC2 + SSO + audit logs + KMS).

Discovery highlights:
- Customer profile: B2B SaaS (product analytics SDK + workspace), NA HQ, EU users require residency.
- Primary workloads: chat (customer-facing assistant in-app), embeddings for search across event data, reranking for top-N results.
- Traffic profile: steady baseline ~150 qps embeddings/search, bursty chat peaks ~400 qps during release windows. Cost-sensitive for bursts.
- Latency targets: chat p95 < 200ms for 512-token flows, embedding gen < 80ms single-vector on avg.
- Model preference: prefer Llama-3-class 70B quantized int8 on Dedicated; acceptable fallback to 13B for lower-cost routes. Want routing policy: prefer low-latency (dedicated region) then fallback to hosted cheaper variant.

POC / eval plan (agreed):
- Phase 1 (hosted, 2 weeks): validate prompt templates, obs via Redwood Console, token-level cost breakdown. (Completed: Feb 2026)

title: Copperleaf Realtime Intelligence
preview: Primary workload: reranking and real-time decisioning for programmatic bids. Targets: sustained 5k rps with burst to 15k rps; p50 < 5ms, p95 < 20ms, p99 < 50ms end-to-end for model decision call. Cost sensitivity: target cost per decision <$0.0006. Model preferences: open weights (Llama2-13B / Mistral-family) with fallback to smaller distilled models. Critical features: per-route batching with latency SLO overrides, prefix/KV caching to reduce repeated token costs, quantization (int8/int4) recommendations but must validate quality on Copperleaf holdout dataset. Security: Okta SSO (SAML), AWS KMS integration, SOC2 compliance, US data residency and audit logs retention for 12 months.

title: Deepwell KnowledgeGrid
preview:
Summary: Large financial analytics provider building RAG over internal research + regulatory filings. Target index 200M–800M vectors initially; plan growth to 1B+ within 12–18 months. Primary workload: embeddings + two-stage rerank (ANN -> cross-attention reranker).

Key constraints: cannot send PII or regulated doc content to third-party public cloud. Customer wants Private deployment or on-prem with customer-managed KMS and full audit logging. Enterprise SLAs required.

Performance & cost targets: p95 rerank latency <=150ms, p99 <=300ms. Throughput target 1,000 qps peak (avg 250 qps). Cost sensitivity: unit cost target $0.0005–$0.002 per query depending on rerank depth; looking to minimize GPU token cost via batching, prefix/KV caching and 8-bit quantization.

POC scope: ingest 50M vectors subset -> validate ANN sharding, hybrid routing, and rerank latency at target QPS; run A/B against in-house baseline for quality vs cost. Deliverables asked: prescriptive batching/caching configs, quantization guidance, cost model for 200M/500M/1B vectors, and a network peering + encryption plan.

Quotes: "Need unit cost under 0.001/token equivalent for reranking at 500M index scale" — Head of Data.

SE notes: Recommend continuous batching with dynamic batch sizing, prefix cache for repeated queries during market opens, and autoscale rules for burst windows. Expect ~40% cache hit on common prefixes. Prefer GPU-backed Dedicated in-region, fallback to Private across other regions for compliance.

Risks: procurement calendar (decision Q2), long security review (legal wants HSM/KMS details), and on-prem infra readiness for 1B vector sharding.

Next steps: send prescriptive config + cost sheet by 2026-03-15; schedule technical deep-dive for peering + KMS on 2026-03-18.
